header.primary_setup: Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT — DC & Harsh Environment displacing legacy FTTH
header.primary_setup_verdict: plausibly present; input confirmed + early output
header.secondary_setup: Corporate Change EPSU/EPT (nascent) — new CEO + decentralised M&A engine
header.conviction: C — forming; small-cap liquidity gate outstanding
header.time_horizon: N1-3Y, fulcrum event inside 12m (BEAD resolution + DC growth re-affirmation)

A.title: FINANCIALS
A.inline_verdict: Light pass — mix shift is the numbers story

rows.label: Group sales (TTM)
rows.delta: -5% y/y organic on legacy, offset by DC +37% & HE +11%

rows.label: Segment mix (2025)
rows.delta: but ~60% of adj. EBITA — the mix-shift story

rows.label: Fibre Solutions margin
rows.delta: down from mid-teens in FTTH peak; cost programme SEK 120m run-rate

rows.label: Customer concentration
rows.delta: KR flag — but diversifying via acquisitions

blocks.text: The numbers at Triaging tell a mix-shift story rather than an absolute-growth story. Group organic was roughly flat in 2025 as Fibre Solutions declined ~5% on overcapacity, offset by DC +37% and HE +11%. What matters is that DC+HE are now a third of sales but ~60% of adjusted EBITA — the legacy business has become dilutive both to growth and to margin.

Financials rate B at Triaging. Not outstanding — the FTTH legacy business is still absorbing working capital (104 DIO) and the performance programme is in early stages. But the mix shift is quantified, the DC unit economics are genuinely asset-light, and there is no cash-generation red flag. Worth ESA depth.

Hexatronic 2026 guidance: targeting __low-double-digit organic growth__ at group level driven primarily by Data Center +25-30% and Harsh Environment +10-15%, partly offset by Fibre Solutions stabilisation around current run-rate. Adjusted EBITA margin guidance: __11-12% group__ (vs 9.8% in 2025), driven by mix-shift toward DC services (asset-light, NWC 12-14%) and SEK 120m run-rate cost savings from the performance programme by end-Q1-26.

Guidance is __plausible but not conservative__ in the way Richard prefers. Management has a mixed track record: beat in 2023, met in 2024, missed in early 2025 before the second-half DC inflection rescued the year. The 2026 framework sits __within the SS consensus range__ (not above it as we'd want for a positive-revision setup). Reading: take guidance at face value at Triaging; pressure-test the 2026 DC growth assumption at ESA depth (KQ for ESA: is +25-30% DC organic actually deliverable given customer concentration in 2-3 hyperscalers?).

SS cluster: 2026e revenue SEK 8.5-9.0bn (vs SEK 7.7bn TTM), EPS SEK 2.10-2.45 (consensus c. SEK 2.25, +20-25% YoY). Wide dispersion on EBITA margin: bears at 10.5% (FTTH overcapacity persists), bulls at 12.5% (DC mix-shift accelerates). Most-recent four broker notes (per AS 06-SS) split 2 buy / 1 hold / 1 sell — sentiment is __cautiously constructive__ post the Q4-25 DC print.

Consensus is __pricing the mix-shift directionally but underweighting the magnitude__ of DC unit economics. The asset-light, low-NWC, 1%-capex profile of DC services means incremental ROIC is materially above group blended — and SS models do not yet decompose this. __Probability of upward EPS revisions__ on FY-26 if Q1-Q2 DC organic prints stay above +25%: __high__ (60-70%). The bear case (FTTH-style boom-bust applied to DC) is the single most-priced concern; if pressure-tested favourably at ESA, this is the catalyst for a re-rating.

subsections.title: Financial forecasts — VF modal case — Watson analysis/judgement

blocks.text: VF modal case (Triaging-depth, illustrative): 2028 revenue SEK 11.0-11.5bn (group CAGR ~13% from 2025), DC SEK 3.0bn (mgmt target), EBITA margin 13-14% (vs SS 11-12%), EPS SEK 3.30-3.60 (vs SS 2.80-3.00). 3-year EPS CAGR ~25%, predominantly mix-driven. __Cumulative dividends + buyback__ over 3 years: c. SEK 4 per share (35% payout assumed).

VF case sits __c. 12-15% above SS on 2028 EPS__ on stronger DC margin trajectory — the main analytical disagreement. Modal case is __not the bull case__: bull case would have DC growing at 35-40% organic for two more years instead of decelerating to 25-30%. The real R/R asymmetry is __upside on multiple re-rate__ (DC services should trade at 15-18x vs the legacy fibre 10-12x) more than on EPS — VF case + re-rate to fair-range mid implies 2-2.5x base case in 24-36 months.

5-year TSR build (Triaging-depth, illustrative): __EPS CAGR ~22% + multiple re-rate ~8% p.a. + yield ~2.5%__ = __c. 32% TSR p.a.__ at modal case. Bear case (DC stalls, multiple stays at 10x): EPS CAGR drops to ~12%, re-rate disappears, TSR drops to ~14% — still positive but no asymmetry. Base/bear gap is the gating decision: __ESA must answer the DC moat question__ (KC-1) to know which case to weight.

TSR breakdown is __re-rate-dependent in a way that requires DC moat conviction__ to justify size. At Triaging size (1.0-1.5%), the asymmetry is acceptable even on the bear case. At target size (2.5-3%), the moat question becomes load-bearing. __Sequencing rule__: do not size up beyond 1.5% before ESA delivers a defensible moat read.

guidance: #J **FY2026 guidance: low-double-digit organic growth + EBITA margin 11-12%.** Mid-point sits within SS consensus range — not the conservative-and-then-beat pattern Richard prefers.
- Revenue: SEK 8.5-9.0bn (+10-13% YoY). EBITA margin: 11-12% (vs 9.8% in FY2025). EPS: SEK 2.10-2.45. #I
- Capital returns: 35% payout maintained. M&A target: 3-6 acquisitions/year in DC, EV<EUR 50m at 5-7x EBITDA. #I
- Guidance midpoint EPS SEK 2.25 = consensus midpoint — no positive surprise embedded. #A
#A **Guidance quality at Triaging:** B-rated. Track record mixed (beat 2023, met 2024, missed early-2025). Worth ESA depth on the 2026 DC growth assumption.
ss_estimates: #J **SS consensus: SEK 8.7bn revenue, EPS SEK 2.25 for FY-26.** Wide dispersion on EBITA margin (bears 10.5% / bulls 12.5%) — the central analytical disagreement is mix-shift magnitude.
- Bull house view: DC scales to 35% group EBITA share by 2027; fair value SEK 320 (40% upside). #I
- Bear house view: DC = FTTH-redux, hyperscaler capex peaks 2026; fair value SEK 180 (-20%). #I
- Central case implies forward P/E 12.5x, well below DC-services peer multiple of 15-18x. #A
#J **SS positioning:** 2 buy / 1 hold / 1 sell. Asymmetry is __upward EPS revision__ (60-70% probability) if Q1-Q2 DC prints sustain +25% organic.

rows.label: 1. CURRENT MULTIPLE: 2YF P/E ~12.5x — well below DC-services peer 15-18x
2. FAIR RANGE: 15th pct. 10x, 30th pct. 13x, 50th pct. 16x, 75th pct. 19x, 90th pct. 22x
3. KEY GATE: DC moat read at ESA determines fair-range location

B.title: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
B.inline_verdict: Plausibly compelling; progress to ESA with a Richard liquidity gate

blocks.text: Progress to ESA. Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT is legibly present and early outputs are showing (DC +37% organic, HE +11%). Two material gates: (i) does the DC niche moat survive Corning/Prysmian pivot if Tier-1 telco demand stalls, and (ii) is Richard comfortable at sub-EUR 1bn liquidity given the Fasadgruppen/XVIVO experience.

The thesis is a mix-shift trade. Hexatronic is pivoting from a commoditised FTTH supplier (where it has ~1% US market share vs Corning/Prysmian/Dura-Line) to a services-led DC and Harsh Environment player. The pivot is measurable: DC+HE revenue share 35% → 40% in one year, EBITA share ~60%. The risk is that the hyperscaler build-out has 'boom-and-bust' characteristics identical to the FTTH cycle that just broke — trading one cyclical trap for another.

items.text: PRIMARY driver: DC segment scaling to SEK 3bn by 2028 — the fulcrum. Proof point is Q4-25 +62% organic.

sub.text: Cloud now ~40% of DC segment sales; hyperscaler concentration is the obvious risk.
Management targets 3-6 acquisitions/year at 5-7x EBITDA, EV <EUR 50m each.

items.text: SECONDARY driver: BEAD programme materialisation — triggers legacy FTTH recovery.

sub.text: $42.5bn US subsidy stalled. Inflection now H2-26 per management.
Rule changes made it 'technology-neutral' — LEO/FWA now eligible. 74% of early-state allocations still go to fibre.

items.text: TERTIARY driver: New CEO Rikard Fröberg execution (from March 2025). Nascent Corporate Change input — too early to rate.

sub.text: Ex-Ansell — international/US experience but no prior fibre/connectivity role.

blocks.text: Setup quality is B rather than A because the DC part of the thesis rhymes too closely with the FTTH boom-and-bust the company is still exiting. If DC is FTTH-in-disguise with a 3-5 year lag, the mix-shift thesis compounds rather than diversifies the cyclical risk. ESA needs to pressure-test this directly — the question is not whether DC growth is real, but whether the revenue quality is durable past the hyperscaler capex cycle.

C.title: REQUIRED ATTRIBUTES
C.inline_verdict: Outputs B-heavy; Foundations OK; FFF has A-rated catalysts

blocks.title: HTRO required-attribute ratings — 22 RAs graded, 2 unclear (Triaging depth)

cells: Flat organic 2025; DC/HE offsetting Fibre decline
Mix shift to 60% EBITA from DC+HE is structural
Depressed cycle base; leverage into recovery
WC drag on Fibre, clean on DC services
DC incremental margins visibly higher than fibre
AI/DC build-out; NATO defence uplift; BEAD latent
1% US share in FTTH vs Corning/Prysmian/Dura-Line
Not vertically integrated in glass — reliance on Corning/Prysmian
Three-pillar pivot is legible and measurable
New CEO from March 2025 — not yet demonstrated

blocks.text: Rollup: Outputs predominantly B, Foundations mixed with A-rated external tailwinds dragged by C-rated legacy competitive position, Management A/C — strategic clarity is the standout, execution evidence is absent by design (new CEO). Two attrs held as 'unclear' — insufficient evidence at Triaging depth, excluded from weighting.

Outputs are a classic mix-shift read. Nothing A-rated because the group is still cycle-depressed; nothing below B except the balance sheet which is acquisition-funded. The interesting line is 4.9 (returns on incremental capital) — DC services is genuinely asset-light with ~1% capex and 12-14% NWC, which drops incremental ROIC meaningfully above the group average.

If the DC segment hits the SEK 3bn 2028 target at 15%+ EBITA, group earnings roughly double from the current trough without Fibre needing to do anything dramatic. The thesis does not require a heroic FTTH recovery — it just requires FTTH to stop getting worse.
P3+P4 at Triaging — full canonical CQ -> RA -> TC structure. PoC content (placeholder text, real signpost coverage).
P3 (Fundamental Change) at Triaging — placeholder.

blocks.text: PoC summary at Triaging: structure mirrors canonical pillar tree; rated tiers and signpost coverage demonstrate the target shape.

Checks clear at Triaging depth. No aggressive accounting flagged in a first-pass read of Q4-25 and 2025 annual disclosures. Segment reporting is adequate. One bearish short report identified (Triaging/10 covers this at ESA depth) — standard FTTH-bear thesis, partly priced, will be pressure-tested at ESA.
Incident history: no material incident flagged. Governance: Swedish listing, no dominant shareholder — double-edged (stewardship thin; activism/takeout optionality up).

Catalyst density is high for a small-cap Nordic industrial. Four identifiable events in the next 12m: DC Q1-26 print (organic trajectory from +62% Q4 base), BEAD state-level disbursements ramping, Harsh Environment project lumpiness resolving, performance programme SEK 120m run-rate landing end-Q1.
Liquidity is the mismatch — EUR 0.8bn market cap, sub-billion. Reassessment trigger flag in master data explicitly references Fasadgruppen/XVIVO lessons. Size starter position accordingly.
Richard gate question: is small-cap/sub-EUR 1bn liquidity a dealbreaker for this name, or a sizing constraint? This is a pre-ESA decision, not an ESA-to-DD decision.

Classification: Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT (primary) + Corporate Change EPSU/EPT (nascent, secondary). Maturity: input confirmed + early output. Lineage: rhymes with multi-segment mix-shift pivots historically (industrial restructurings where a new division's incremental economics rerate the whole group). False-friend risk rated low-moderate — the distinct risk is reading DC acceleration as a durable moat when it may be a hyperscaler-capex-cycle phenomenon.

D.title: COMPONENTS
D.inline_verdict: Mechanics legible; two ACHs around DC cyclicality to test at ESA

subsections.title: KDICs — Key Drivers of Investment Case

items.text: **__demibold__KDIC-1 — DC segment scale-up to SEK 3bn by 2028.__** THE fulcrum driver. Q4-25 proof point: __DC organic +62%__ (vs guide +35%); group EBITA share already __60%__ from __40%__ revenue. Conviction: __high__ (mgmt explicit target, supported by hyperscaler capex visibility).

**__demibold__KDIC-2 — BEAD programme materialisation.__** Secondary driver — triggers Fibre Solutions recovery from -5% organic to flat/low-positive. Conviction: __medium__ (federal allocation in place; state-level disbursement pace uncertain through 2026).

**__demibold__KDIC-3 — CEO Rikard Fröberg execution evidence (from Mar-25).__** Tertiary driver — strategy clarity is A-rated; execution evidence still nascent. Conviction: __low-medium__ at Triaging (insufficient time-in-seat for proof points).
**KHF-1 — AI/hyperscaler capex cycle.** __Structurally supportive for N1-3Y__ (cloud + AI infra build-out); cyclicality risk emerges past 3Y if hyperscaler capex normalises.
**KHF-2 — NATO defence budget growth.** Supports Harsh Environment +10-15% organic; assumption is durable through 2027 given current geopolitical posture.
**KHF-3 — Performance programme delivery.** SEK 120m run-rate cost savings landing end-Q1-26 — __load-bearing for 2026 margin guidance__.
**KC-1 — Does the DC niche moat (end-to-end + installer time savings) hold if Corning/Prysmian pivot in?** __THE binary check__ for ESA. If moat is breached, DC is FTTH-redux with 3-5y lag.

**KC-2 — Customer concentration durability.** Top-10 = ~50% of group sales; mgmt is broadening base. Question: how fast is concentration declining and is it actually a problem given hyperscaler stickiness?
**KU-1 — M&A pipeline pricing in 2026.** Are 5-7x EBITDA targets actually still available, or has PE caught on?
**KR-1 — Customer concentration.** Top-10 = ~50% of sales. Sensitive to one large hyperscaler delaying or cancelling a build.
**KR-2 — Supply chain (not vertically integrated in glass).** Margin exposure to glass fibre price spikes; less controllable than Corning's integrated model.
**KR-3 — Integration complexity from M&A engine.** 3-6 acquisitions/year is operationally demanding for a SEK 9bn market-cap company.
**KPO-1 — CEO execution proof point in first 12m.** Successful first material strategic move would re-rate the management quality score.
**KPO-2 — M&A pipeline delivery at disciplined multiples.** Each accretive deal at 5-7x EBITDA compounds the DC scale story.
**KPO-3 — BEAD state-level award flow.** Each major state award materially shifts the Fibre Solutions recovery trajectory.

blocks.text: _{10_INVALIDATION_ACHS} **Live-case invalidation discipline.** These ten one-strike rules apply __while we own this case__ and are explicitly different from the screening thresholds used for new investments. Any single trip → __APM escalates to Richard__ with a sell/trim recommendation; no waiting for a second confirmation. State of each threshold is tracked below._
**Single-event invalidations (rules 1–5)** — fundamental triggers that fire on a single condition.

items.text: **1. Top-line invalidation** — One __probable or actual near-term revenue cut__ to SS/G caused by an exogenous problem.
**2. Cockroach invalidation** — __One actual AND one probable__, OR __three probable/likely__ current/near-term problems (cause internal or exogenous; impact on profits or revenue).
**3. Ditherer invalidation** — __Deterioration in operator assessment__ AND one or more current/near-term probable/actual problems (cause internal or exogenous; impact on profits or revenue).
**4. Cyclical invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY a __plausible+ near-term revenue cut__ caused by a threshold+ exogenous problem.
**5. NT/MT one-two invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any __probable/actual near-term cut__ to profits (revenue is covered by #4).

blocks.text: **SP-conditional & composite invalidations (rules 6–10)** — sequence-dependent triggers (price action × fundamentals).

items.text: **6. Wisdom of crowd invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any plausible+ VF or actual SM threshold+ concerns re. __mid/long-term growth, margins, SRCAs__ or predictability thereof.

**7. Market catch-up with our existential concerns invalidation** — __VF (not SM) plausible+ threshold+ concerns__ re. mid/long-term growth, margins, SRCAs or predictability thereof FOLLOWED BY __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__.
**8. Narrow frame invalidation** — __Peerset underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any __actual cut/problem__ (cause internal or exogenous; impact on profits or revenue).
**9. SS EEG invalidation** — __[2]% or greater SS EPS cuts__ AND __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ (either order).
**10. Case outputs/attributes invalidation** — __[8] or more case-output thresholds at D or F.__

blocks.text: _Escalation protocol: when a threshold trips, APM (i) records the trip in the case file with date and evidence, (ii) re-rates the affected pillar(s), (iii) escalates with __trigger evidence, thesis impact, recommended action__ (sell / trim X% / hold-with-tighter-stop) and reasoning chain, and (iv) flips the state in this section to TRIPPED. This sits __upstream of__ the 30-Day Shot Clock — the shot clock applies for deterioration without a tripped threshold; once a threshold trips, APM acts._

Beyond the 10 standard ACHs, HTRO-specific exit triggers per master data: __Minervini score <7/8 sustained for 2+ months__ (technical breakdown signal); __DC quarterly organic growth <20% for 2 consecutive quarters__ (mix-shift thesis breaks); __BEAD programme cancelled or delayed >24 months__ (Fibre recovery cancelled); __value-destructive M&A__ (paying >9x EBITDA for marginal DC asset). Any one of these triggers a full re-assessment, not necessarily an immediate exit.

subsections.title: Negative, should-be-expected developments — Watson note on coverage gaps

blocks.text: **Negative developments to be expected (do not constitute thesis breaks):** 1-2 quarters of DC growth deceleration are very likely across a 24m hold (mean-reversion from +62% Q4-25 base). Fibre Solutions printing flat or down -2-3% in any quarter through 2026 is consistent with the thesis. Short-seller activity flagged in AS 10-Short — standard FTTH-bear thesis, partly priced; do not trade on noise.

**Coverage gaps flagged at Triaging:** RESEARCHER 03-TM (Technical Momentum) is missing — P1 score is held at low confidence. RESEARCHER 13-KQ (canonical Key Questions template) not yet authored — D.III.1 below uses 14-KD as proxy. Both gaps queued in `memory/staging/researcher-queue.md` (22-Apr-26 entry). Triaging judgement is **defensible without these inputs** but ESA must close them before sizing decision.

items.text: **KOQ-1 — Is the DC niche moat durable against Corning/Prysmian tier-1 incursion?** ESA top-priority. Required answer: defensible Yes/No with specific moat mechanics.
**KOQ-2 — Fasadgruppen / XVIVO comparison: small-cap (EUR 0.8bn) liquidity comfort for Richard's portfolio?** Reassessment trigger flag in master data references these explicitly.
**KOQ-3 — M&A pipeline quality in 2026.** Are deals actually available at 5-7x EBITDA, or has PE dry powder compressed multiples?
**KA-1 — Run RESEARCHER 03-TM and 13-KQ.** Close coverage gaps flagged in D.II.3.
**KA-2 — Pressure-test the 'DC = FTTH-redux' bear thesis** in a directed AS deep-research query (target: Q1 customer-concentration analysis + Corning/Prysmian competitive overlap).
**KA-3 — Run small-cap liquidity overlay** (3m ADV, ADR/GDR availability, borrow availability, drawdown tolerance per Fasadgruppen lesson).

E.title: ACTIONS
E.inline_verdict: Progress to ESA subject to small-cap gate

blocks.text: Progress to ESA. Setup is legible (Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT with Corporate Change overlay), PRIMARY driver is clean and measurable (DC scaling), attribute ratings are mixed B-heavy with A-rated external tailwinds. One gate before ESA: Richard to confirm small-cap comfort given Fasadgruppen/XVIVO experience.
If the DC moat question (KC-1) comes back negative at ESA — i.e. end-to-end differentiation does not survive Corning/Prysmian tier-1 pivot — park. This is a binary check the ESA work must answer.

items.text: Initial size: 1.0-1.5% (half a standard starter given EUR 0.8bn market cap).
Build to 2.5-3% on first confirming DC print + M&A deal at claimed multiples.
Pair-trade optionality against a large-cap fibre peer if available — defer decision to ESA.
Park [-] with 6m re-review trigger on next two DC prints + BEAD disbursement trajectory.
Keep CARLB / CRBN / HTWS as alternative fibre-adjacent infrastructure expressions (no gap in coverage).

F.inline_verdict: Light at Triaging per stage-depth matrix
